title: Fuel and emission control structure description - ECU control

blocks.text: Fuel and emission control structure description - ECU control

The PGM-FI ECU consists of a CPU (central processing unit), memory (memory unit), and I/O (input/output unit) and performs fuel injection control, ignition timing control, idle control control, and DBW control.

If normal control is continued while the PGM-FI ECU detects an abnormality, there is a possibility that it will cause problems such as deteriorated driving performance and emissions. At this time, the PGM-FI ECU replaces the signal in which an abnormality is detected with a preset value and controls as a fail-safe function. This control enables minimum driving in the event of a breakdown.
The PGM-FI ECU is equipped with an overwritable ROM; it can be updated to the latest program by using the MVCI and communicating with the PGM-FI ECU.
Even after the ignition is turned to the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode, the PGM-FI ECU is still powered on to monitor the vehicle status for a certain period of time.
To improve workability and reduce weight by shortening the wire harness, the PGM-FI ECU is located in the engine compartment near the air cleaner.

To ensure operation in the engine compartment environment, the PGM-FI ECU itself and the coupler section are waterproof. Therefore, when performing circuit inspections in the ECU coupler section, such as failure diagnosis work, it is necessary to make measurements on the terminal side of the coupler, not on the cord side of the coupler.
